The thesis is a fresh Trump-Apple catalyst driving a clean 12% 5-day surge with the regime tailwind. The one thing that kills this: that 0.27 relative volume reading — if institutions don't show up to confirm, the spike fades fast. Go half size until volume validates.

synthesis.summary: CVNA is up 6.4% today, likely on a read-through from CarMax's Q1 earnings beat, which investors interpreted as a positive signal for the broader used-car market. However, the CarMax news is about a competitor and analysts still want more proof of a sustained turnaround, so it only partially accounts for CVNA's move — notably, relative volume is well below average (0.38x), suggesting this isn't a high-conviction institutional drive.

Kroger dropped sharply after reporting missed earnings estimates, compressed margins, and issuing a softer-than-expected forward outlook — a combination that signals both near-term execution problems and deteriorating profitability. The stock is deeply oversold (RSI 29) with a bearish MACD cross, suggesting the selloff has been sustained across multiple sessions, consistent with the 5-day loss of over 9%.

SNDK is up 11% with no high-impact news in the supplied set to explain the move. The only available news item is a low-impact broad market round-up that mentions other tickers, not SNDK specifically. The move — with RSI at 70.7 and relatively low relative volume (0.58) — is more consistent with options flow, short covering, or sector rotation in memory/storage stocks than any identifiable catalyst.

Marvell and Tower Semiconductor announced they have shipped over 5 million photonic chips for AI data centers, a concrete volume milestone that signals accelerating demand for MRVL's silicon photonics products. This directly ties Marvell's revenue outlook to the AI infrastructure buildout, which likely drove the ~12% single-day surge. However, relative volume (0.69) is below average, suggesting options flow or pre-positioned buying may also be contributing to the magnitude of the move.

EQPT is up 13% on the day and 35% over the past week with no high-impact news in the supplied feed to explain the move. The only relevant item is a low-impact Benzinga roundup that mentions other tickers, not EQPT directly. The below-average relative volume (0.7) further suggests this isn't driven by broad retail participation, pointing more toward options flow, thin float dynamics, or undisclosed catalyst.

CarMax jumped 6.5% after reporting a Q1 earnings beat, which triggered a relief rally. However, the analyst coverage suggests the market isn't fully convinced of a sustained turnaround, meaning the move reflects an initial positive earnings reaction rather than a broader re-rating of the business.

QuantumScape surged ~15% after announcing a joint research agreement with Honda to develop solid-state battery technology, signaling a major new corporate partner validating QS's technology and expanding its commercialization pathway beyond its existing Volkswagen relationship.

VSH is up 10.6% on elevated RSI and a significant single-day move, but none of the supplied news items address Vishay Intertechnology specifically — the only news is a generic market movers roundup with no VSH-specific catalyst. The move is unexplained by available news; likely drivers could include options flow, earnings-related speculation, or sector rotation in electronic components, none of which are documented here.

INTC surged 8.57% after Trump announced an Apple partnership involving Intel, directly driving the outsized single-day move. Volume was notably subdued (rel_volume 0.27), suggesting the gain was driven by price discovery rather than broad institutional participation, but the news catalyst is clear and proportionate to the move.
